en.extract:
The rosy-patched bushshrike (Rhodophoneus cruentus) is a species of bird in the family Malaconotidae. It is the only species placed in the genus Rhodophoneus. It is found in Djibouti, Egypt, Eritrea, Ethiopia, Kenya, Somalia, Sudan, and Tanzania. Its natural habitat is subtropical or tropical dry shrubland.


Taxonomy
The rosy-patched bushshrike was formally described in 1828 as Lanius cruentus by the German naturalists Wilhelm Hemprich and Christian Gottfried Ehrenberg based on specimens collected at Arkiko near Massawa in Eritrea. The specific epithet is Latin meaning "bloody" or "stained with blood". It is now the only species placed in the genus Rhodophoneus which was introduced in 1871 by the German explorer and ornithologist Theodor von Heuglin to accommodate a single species, the rosy-patched bushshrike. The genus name combines the Ancient Greek ῥοδον/rhodon meaning "rose" with φονευς/phoneus meaning "murderer".
Four subspecies are recognised:

R. c. kordofanicus Sclater, WL & Mackworth-Praed, CW, 1918 – west-central Sudan
R. c. cruentus (Hemprich, WF & Ehrenberg, CG, 1828) – northeastern Sudan, Eritrea, and northern Ethiopia
R. c. hilgerti (Neumann, OR, 1903) – far southeastern South Sudan, southern and eastern Ethiopia, Djibouti, Somalia, and northern and eastern Kenya
R. c. cathemagmenus (Reichenow, A, 1887) – southern Kenya (Tsavo and Lake Victoria) to northeastern Tanzania

Jr | year=1960 | title=Check-List of Birds of the World | volume=9 | publisher=Museum of Comparative Zoology | location=Cambridge, Massachusetts | page=320 | url=https://www.biodiversitylibrary.org/page/14480995 }}{{ cite web | author=AviList Core Team | date=2025 | title=AviList: The Global Avian Checklist, v2025 | doi=10.2173/avilist.v2025 | doi-access=free | url=http://www.avilist.org/checklist/v2025/ | access-date=16 November 2025 }} The genus name combines the [[Ancient Greek]] ῥοδον/''rhodon'' meaning "rose" with φονευς/''phoneus'' meaning "murderer".{{ cite web | last=Jobling | first=James A. | title=Rhodophoneus | work=The Key to Scientific Names | url=https://birdsoftheworld.org/bow/key-to-scientific-names/search?q=Rhodophoneus | publisher=Cornell Lab of Ornithology | access-date=16 November 2025 }} Four [[subspecies]] are recognised: * ''R. c. kordofanicus'' [[William Lutley Sclater|Sclater, WL]] & [[Cyril Mackworth-Praed|Mackworth-Praed, CW]], 1918 – west-central Sudan * ''R. c. cruentus'' ([[Wilhelm Hemprich|Hemprich, WF]] & [[Christian Gottfried Ehrenberg|Ehrenberg, CG]], 1828) – northeastern Sudan, [[Eritrea]], and northern Ethiopia * ''R. c. hilgerti'' ([[Oscar Neumann|Neumann, OR]], 1903) – far southeastern South Sudan, southern and eastern Ethiopia, [[Djibouti]], Somalia, and northern and eastern Kenya * ''R. c. cathemagmenus'' ([[Anton Reichenow|Reichenow, A]], 1887) – southern Kenya ([[Tsavo]] and [[Lake Victoria]]) to northeastern Tanzania ==Gallery== Image:Rosy-patched Bushshrike female specimen RWD.jpg|Rosy-patched shrike (female) specimen
